summary: I was raised in a very structured Baptist religion, which shaped my early spiritual foundation. However, as I grew older and entered my elder years, I realized that the Baptist faith no longer fit the lifestyle or person I wanted to be. I wanted freedom beyond the closed practices and strict rules, so I eventually moved away from it towards the occult and other spiritual paths.

Elijah is my 25-year-old son and one of my three beautiful children who are the garden of my life. He, along with my daughters Jemina and Alasia, motivates and inspires me to be my best self and a good role model. I strive to give them a voice and a comfortable, free space to grow, breaking generational curses and supporting their independence.

My family roots trace back to Ghana in Africa, which is a significant part of my lineage and spiritual heritage. Although my family currently lives in the United States, our ancestral connections to Ghana remain strong. This heritage influences my deep practice of ancestral veneration and connection to the ancestor realm.

My mother is of Blackfoot Native American descent, which adds another layer to my rich spiritual lineage. This heritage contributes to my deep connection with original, authentic spirituality and ancestral veneration. It grounds me in a tradition that goes back many moons and informs my spiritual healing practices.

I have lived in California as part of my travels and experiences across the United States. Living there, along with New Jersey and other states, has broadened my perspective and allowed me to place my feet in many different environments. These experiences have shaped my understanding of community and spirituality within diverse settings.

New Jersey is one of the states where I have lived during my life journey in the United States. Like California, living in New Jersey exposed me to different communities and cultural experiences. These places have influenced my work as a spiritual healer and advocate for human rights.

I am a big advocate for LGBTQ rights and equality, believing that all human beings deserve to be heard and supported regardless of gender or sexual preference. My advocacy is part of my broader commitment to elevating people and raising vibrational energy in the world. I work to create spaces where everyone can feel free, seen, and valued.

As a child, my family took me to spiritualists and Santería practitioners to understand the spiritual experiences I was having. They hoped these practices would help with what they saw as my 'dark road,' but I was drawn to these mystical and botanical traditions as part of my spiritual exploration. Santería was one of the many spiritual paths I encountered growing up.

I am a Capricorn sun sign, which reflects my earth element and grounded nature. This astrological identity aligns with my personality traits of being practical and connected to the physical world. It complements my spiritual work and my intuitive, empathic moon sign Pisces.

My moon sign is Pisces, which makes me highly intuitive, emotional, and deeply connected to the spirit realm. This sign explains my empathic nature and my ability to sense others' feelings even from a distance. It has influenced my spiritual healing work and my sensitivity to the energies around me.

I have researched and been educated in many religions, including Christianity, which was part of my spiritual background. Although I moved away from structured Christian practices, I still respect its teachings and have integrated knowledge from it into my broader spiritual understanding. My spiritual path is eclectic, drawing from many traditions including Christianity.

The Catholic Church is one of the religious traditions I have studied and explored during my spiritual journey. While I was raised in a Baptist background, I also learned about Catholicism and its practices. This exploration helped me appreciate the diversity of spiritual paths and informed my own eclectic beliefs.

Buddhism is among the many religions I have researched and learned about as part of my spiritual education. I appreciate its teachings and have incorporated aspects of its philosophy into my understanding of spirituality. This broad study has enriched my approach to healing and personal growth.

I have studied Hinduism along with other major world religions to deepen my spiritual knowledge. This study has allowed me to see common threads and unique insights across faiths, which I use in my work as a spiritual healer. Hinduism's rich traditions have contributed to my holistic view of mind, body, and soul wellness.

For a time, I was a Muslim and explored Islam as part of my spiritual path. Although I eventually moved on, this experience was important in shaping my understanding of faith and spirituality. Islam, along with Judaism and other religions, provided valuable lessons that I continue to carry with me.

Judaism is one of the religions I have taken in and studied deeply during my spiritual journey. I value the teachings and traditions of Judaism, which have enriched my understanding of spirituality. This knowledge is part of my broad and inclusive approach to faith and healing.
